diagrams.title: Shortest Path Example
diagrams.description: A weighted graph showing multiple paths between vertices A and E, with the shortest path highlighted in a different color. Shows how different routes have different total weights.
elements: vertices A, B, C, D, E as circles
highlighted shortest path A-B-D-E with total weight 9
alternative longer paths shown in different style
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the concept of finding the minimum weight path between two vertices in a weighted graph

diagrams.title: Spanning Tree from Graph
diagrams.description: A graph with a cycle on the left transforming into a spanning tree on the right by removing one edge from the cycle. Shows that a spanning tree has n-1 edges and no cycles.
elements: original graph with 5 vertices and cycles
labels showing vertex count and edge count
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates what a spanning tree is and how it differs from a general graph by having no cycles

diagrams.title: Minimum Spanning Tree (MST)
diagrams.description: A weighted graph with multiple edges, showing the Minimum Spanning Tree highlighted in bold. Demonstrates connecting all vertices with minimum total weight.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the Minimum Spanning Tree concept - connecting all vertices with minimum total edge weight, a key algorithm problem
